reason: Both are arXiv /list/recent pages for different CS subject categories; identical dt/dd row count (50) and tag structure.
Replay is a single paper abstract detail page (/abs/ URL, long title, 0 rows, no dt/dd) vs a listing of 50 papers.
Replay is a single book detail page (0 rows, tr/th/td spec-table shape, title is one book name) vs a product-grid listing.
Both are HN story-listing pages using the same table template; /ask is a filtered front-page list (23 rows), same kind as /news (30 rows).
Both are Lobsters story-listing pages (25 rows each); /newest uses the identical template as the front page.
Replay is a user profile page (/~ URL, 0 rows, tiny tag counts with a single h1/section/img/time) vs a 25-row story listing.
Replay is a single package detail page (0 rows, massive div/p/a/img/time counts from readme and changelog) vs a 20-result search listing.
Replay is a tag-filtered quote listing (/tag/love/), same 10 rows and near-identical tag structure as the homepage quote list.
Replay is another subreddit top-listing page with matching body_class 'listing-page top-page'; row drop (25→5) is a login-wall artifact, not a page-kind change.
Replay is a comments thread (body_class 'single-page comments-page', 1 row, full-sentence news headline title) vs a subreddit listing.
Replay is a raw Atom/XML feed endpoint (&output=atom), returning a single <pre> tag with 0 rows and no title — not an HTML filing-list page.
Replay is a tag-filtered questions list (/questions/tagged/javascript) with 15 rows and near-identical tag counts to the unfiltered /questions listing.
Replay is the tablets product-grid listing (21 rows); same e-commerce template as laptops (117 rows), tag ratios scale proportionally.
Replay is the site root/category-nav page (3 rows, tag counts dramatically deflated vs 117-row product grid); the matched rows are category tiles, not product cards.
Replay is a Wikipedia article page (ns-0 body class, 0 rows, massive cite/sup/i counts) vs a category listing page (ns-14).
Both are Wikipedia category pages (ns-14 body class) listing films; same div/span/a/li/ul/h3/bdi/nav tag structure, row difference reflects category size.
Both are Wiktionary category pages (ns-14, 200 rows each); identical td:681/tr:33 structural fingerprint confirms same template.
Replay is a dictionary word-entry article (ns-0 body class, 0 rows, massively inflated span/abbr/b/p/i/dd counts) vs a 200-entry category listing.
